channel.title: [SEEKING GREENLIGHTS] for SCP idea
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "[SEEKING GREENLIGHTS] for SCP idea" - A pokémon video game where every NPC is self aware, and the ethical dilema of dealing with their society

item.title: [SEEKING GREENLIGHTS] for SCP idea
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre: I'm not sure

Page Layout: A normal article, with several addendums

Elevator Pitch: The inhabitants of the world split into two factions and are about to get into a war. The player could stop it, since being the protagonist of the game the inhabitants of it treat him like a celebrity.

This presents a dilema to the Foundation: Should they interviene? If the people inside of the game are to be considered human, should the Foundation policy of political neutrality apply?

Central Narrative: A cartridge of Pokémon HeartGold where every NPC is sentient and self-aware. Every inhabitant of the world lives a life on their own, without knowing they are inside of a video game. The player cannot really communicate with them (since that's not an option in Pokémon games), but the Foundation can monitor their society by talking to the NPCs, whose dialog changes regularly.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: I think a mini Pokémon world living inside a cartridge is a pretty good concept which hasn't been tried before.

Additional Notes: I feel the article lacks a central narrative to tie it all together. I may need help with that.
